claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 9 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 9 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 9 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'm happy to help you determine your eligibility for Child Benefit.

To get started, I'll need to gather some information about you and your children. Let me begin with a few questions.

**First, how many children do you have that you'd like me to assess for Child Benefit eligibility?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! I have one child I'd like to check eligibility for. Their name is Alex, and they're 9 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! It's helpful to know you have one child, Alex, who is 9 years old.

Before I can assess eligibility, I need to understand the rules better. Let me read the official guidance on Child Benefit eligibility.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! I've reviewed the eligibility rules. Now I need to ask you a few questions about your specific circumstances to determine if you qualify for Child Benefit for Alex.

**Do you live with Alex, or are you responsible for Alex in another way?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that. That's helpful.

Now, I need to understand your own circumstances to complete the eligibility assessment.

**Are you a UK resident, and do you live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK. I'm in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you. That's good to know.

Now I have one more important question about your immigration status or circumstances:

**Do you have the right to reside in the UK? For example, are you a British citizen, or do you have settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, or another type of visa or status that allows you to reside here?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I'm a British citizen, so I have the right to reside here.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect! Thank you for that information. I now have all the details I need to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Based on the information you've provided:

- **Alex is 9 years old** (under 16, which is a key requirement)
- **Alex lives with you** (you are responsible for them)
- **You live in the UK** (in Manchester)
- **You are a British citizen** (you have the right to reside in the UK)

Let me now make my eligibility determination:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Great news! You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.**

You meet all the key eligibility requirements:
- Alex is 9 years old (under 16)
- Alex lives with you
- You live in the UK
- You have the right to reside in the UK as a British citizen

You should now be able to make a claim for Child Benefit through HMRC. If you have any further questions or your circumstances change, feel free to contact the Child Benefit Office or HMRC for guidance on the next steps.

reasonings: Alex is eligible for Child Benefit. According to the eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK. In this case: (1) Alex is 9 years old, which is under 16; (2) Alex lives with you, meaning you are responsible for them; (3) You live in the UK (Manchester); (4) You are a British citizen with the right to reside in the UK. All key eligibility criteria are met.
arguments.overall_reasoning: You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex. You meet all the basic requirements: you are responsible for a child under 16, you live together in the UK, and you have the right to reside in the UK as a British citizen. You should be able to proceed with your claim through HMRC."
